title: Using a Live Analysis System to Study Amyloplast Replication in Arabidopsis Ovule Integuments
author: Makoto T. Fujiwara
license_note: PMC full-text XML — Bio-protocol — CC-BY confirmed in article XML

steps.title: Seeds of transgenic Arabidopsis lines with...
steps.instruction: Seeds of transgenic Arabidopsis lines with stable expression of stroma-targeted fluorescent proteins (see General note 1). This protocol presents data from transgenic lines in the Col background [8] and minE [9], and ftsZ triple [10] (hereafter, ftsZ ) mutants, in which a transit peptide (the N-terminal 90 aa of AtFtsZ1-1 [8])-fused YFP is driven by the constitutive CaMV 35S promoter (see Figure 1 ) Figure

steps.title: Reproductive stages of Arabidopsis suitable for analysis of amyloplast replication
steps.instruction: Reproductive stages of Arabidopsis suitable for analysis of amyloplast replication. (A) Primary inflorescence. (B) Flower stages 14–17 [11] used for ovule extraction. (C) Expression pattern of CaMV 35S promoter-driven stroma-targeted YFP in a transgenic Arabidopsis line. Images of brightfield (BF) and yellow fluorescent protein (YFP) signals are shown. Scale bar, 5 mm. Reagents

steps.title: Sow seeds on Murashige and Skoog medium (pH 5
steps.instruction: Sow seeds on Murashige and Skoog medium (pH 5.

steps.title: supplemented with 0
steps.instruction: supplemented with 0.1% MES, 2% sucrose, and 0.7% agar (see General note 2). Treat the seeds in the dark at 4 °C for several days [9]. Germination occurs in one or several days after irradiation at 23 °C. After germination, transfer seedlings to Jiffy-7 peat pellets pre-soaked in water and grow under a 16/8 light/dark cycle of white light illumination at 23 °C for 5–6 weeks (see General note 2; Figure 1 ).

steps.title: Prepare two glass slides
steps.instruction: Prepare two glass slides. One (slide

steps.title: is used for ovule extraction
steps.instruction: is used for ovule extraction. The other (slide 2), together with 1–3 layers of colored tape as a spacer, is used for microscopy analysis (see General note 3).

steps.title: Excise an intact flower or silique...
steps.instruction: Excise an intact flower or silique from an inflorescence stem of an adult plant (see General note 4).

steps.title: Place the flower or silique (segment) on slide
steps.instruction: Place the flower or silique (segment) on slide

steps.title: Fix the organ in place using...
steps.instruction: Fix the organ in place using surgical tape by securing the pedicle at one point so that it does not move during dissection ( Figure 2A –E). Figure

steps.title: Extraction of ovules for microscopic analysis
steps.instruction: Extraction of ovules for microscopic analysis. (A,B) Sampling and dissection of a silique. (C,D) Sampling and dissection of a pistil from an open flower. (E) Slides used for dissection (top) and microscopy (bottom). (F) An extracted septum–ovule association (arrow) mounted in water. (G) 60× objective used for microscopy. (H, I) Ovules observed under 10× (H) and 20× (I) objectives. Scale bars, 2 mm (A–D, F) and 100 μm (H, I).

steps.title: To extract ovules from a silique...
steps.instruction: To extract ovules from a silique at flower stages 16–17, remove sepals, petals, and stamens from the silique, if attached, using basic tweezers (tweezers

steps.title: under a stereomicroscope at zoom 2
steps.instruction: under a stereomicroscope at zoom 2.0–3.2×. Peel off either or both valves (this is optional) from the silique ( Figure 2A , B).

steps.title: To extract ovules from a pistil...
steps.instruction: To extract ovules from a pistil at flower stages 13–16, remove sepals, petals, and stamens from an open flower using extra-fine tweezers (tweezers

steps.title: under a stereomicroscope at zoom 6
steps.instruction: under a stereomicroscope at zoom 6.3×. Peel off either or both valves (this is optional) from the pistil ( Figure 2C , D).

steps.title: Apply 40–70 µL of deionized water...
steps.instruction: Apply 40–70 µL of deionized water or liquid Murashige and Skoog medium onto slide

steps.title: Transfer a septum–ovule association obtained by...
steps.instruction: Transfer a septum–ovule association obtained by dissection to the water drop ( Figure 2F ).

steps.title: Mount a coverslip carefully onto the...
steps.instruction: Mount a coverslip carefully onto the slide (see General note 5). Gently apply the mounting medium between the coverslip and the slide, so that it just fills the space between them ( Figure 2E ).

steps.title: Confirm the extraction of intact ovules...
steps.instruction: Confirm the extraction of intact ovules by low-magnification light microscopy ( Figure 2H , I).

steps.title: Observe amyloplasts in outer ovule integument...
steps.instruction: Observe amyloplasts in outer ovule integument (epidermal or oi2 [12]) cells by high-magnification fluorescence microscopy using a 60×/1.20 NA water-immersion objective ( Figure 2G ; see section B). B. Observation of amyloplasts by conventional fluorescence microscopy

steps.title: Turn on the halogen lamp, mercury...
steps.instruction: Turn on the halogen lamp, mercury lamp, camera controller (computer), and digital camera.

steps.title: Place slide 2 on the stage...
steps.instruction: Place slide 2 on the stage of the inverted fluorescence microscope.

steps.title: To perform counting and morphological characterization...
steps.instruction: To perform counting and morphological characterization of amyloplasts, use a 60× water-immersion objective ( Figure 2G ). Microscope settings for the detection of YFP are described in Table 1 .

steps.title: Start the camera controller software HCImage (Hamamatsu Photonics)
steps.instruction: Start the camera controller software HCImage (Hamamatsu Photonics). The settings of the mercury lamp and camera for the detection of YFP fluorescence are described in Table 2 .

steps.title: Explore and focus on individual ovules...
steps.instruction: Explore and focus on individual ovules at 60× through eyepieces using differential interference contrast (DIC) optics.

steps.title: Perform image acquisition ( Figures 3 –5)
steps.instruction: Perform image acquisition ( Figures 3 –5). For time-lapse fluorescence microscopy, we manually focus on individual cells and capture images for up to 60 min. Each image is captured every 30 s or 1 min. Table

description: Validation of protocol This protocol, or parts of it, has been used and validated in the following research article: Fujiwara et al. [7]. Principles of amyloplast replication in the ovule integuments of Arabidopsis thaliana. Plant Physiology (Figure 1F–K, M–O; Figure 2A–D; Figure 4A–D; Figure 5A–E; and Supplementary figures).
